positioning: Obesity Killer Kit is a doctor-formulated Ayurvedic obesity and weight-loss support system.
Core positioning boundary: structured, guided protocol/system; not a loose supplement and not positioned as a standalone fat-burner claim product.
Mechanism type: protocol-based appetite regulation + eating-behavior correction + digestion support.
Claim posture allowed: supports weight-loss efforts by making adherence easier in real life (reduced unnecessary intake -> improved caloric-deficit adherence -> body uses stored fat over time).
promise_claims: Core promise: 3 to 5 kg weight loss in 15 days when protocol is followed.
eligibility: Primary user: adults with BMI above 25 who want fast visible weight-loss support with natural and practical adherence.
exclusions: Not for: women within 6 months postpartum.
protocol_morning: Take OK Liquid in the morning on an empty stomach (first thing on waking).
After OK Liquid: keep a 4-hour no-solid-food window.
protocol_night: Take OK Tablet at night around 15 minutes before sleep.
Take OK Powder at night with tablet: 1/2 tsp OK Powder around 15 minutes before sleep with warm water.
FAQ note: typically after dinner gap, with warm water guidance.
fasting_rules: Routine logic: eat only on true hunger; no fixed meal timing.
Many users shift to 1 to 1.5 meals/day (as an observed routine outcome/shift).
allowed_items: Zero-calorie drinks allowed during the 4-hour no-solids window.
Diet allowed: vegetables, salads, soups, fruits.
If still hungry: up to 30g complex carbs (millets or oats).
Dairy exception noted: dairy restricted except milk in tea/coffee.
restricted_items: Diet restricted: dairy except milk in tea/coffee.
mechanism_allowed_claims: OK Liquid (morning): satiety support / fullness support; craving reduction; helps shift from clock-based eating to true-hunger eating; supports lower unnecessary intake without hunger-led weakness.
OK Tablet + OK Powder (night): digestive comfort support; helps with bloating, acidity, gas, heaviness; supports digestion, absorption, and elimination comfort.
System effect: reduced meal frequency, reduced portion size, easier routine consistency.
Core flow: lower unnecessary intake -> improved caloric-deficit adherence -> body uses stored fat over time.
mechanism_disallowed_claims: Not allowed: positioned as a standalone fat-burner claim product.
safety_public_line: Safety framing (public claim): no side effects.
safety_internal_handling: Internal handling: first 3 days can be an adjustment phase.
guarantee: Refund/guarantee: if weight-loss start is not observed in the first 7 days, refund applies only when protocol is followed and daily tracker is shared.
continuation: Continuation: continue in cycles until target weight or BMI 25.
After every 30 days: take a 7 to 10 day break.
maintenance: Maintenance: maintain caloric balance, balanced diet, daily activity.
No automatic bounce back from stopping alone (maintenance requires diet/activity/booklet guidance framing).
messaging_guardrails: Guided support should be defined as tracker-led guidance + WhatsApp follow-up + coach support (not vague emotional-support copy).
Do not make homemade-food compatibility the center of brand positioning even when used as a benefit lane.
Do not let secondary benefits overshadow the main promise of fast visible weight loss with a safer, easier, more guided experience.
on_image_copy_requirements: ON-IMAGE COPY MANDATE (NON-NEGOTIABLE): mechanism messaging must explicitly connect to obesity/weight-loss goal; not abstract behavior language alone.
ON-IMAGE COPY MANDATE (NON-NEGOTIABLE): every headline/subheadline pair must make weight loss, obesity reduction, or a clear weight outcome unmistakable.
ON-IMAGE COPY MANDATE (NON-NEGOTIABLE): do not use vague transformational lines without naming weight loss or obesity context.

ON-IMAGE COPY MANDATE (NON-NEGOTIABLE): approved explicit phrases include weight loss support, obesity management support, lose weight, reduce excess weight, 3 to 5 kg in 15 days, appetite control for weight loss.

Headline/subheadline rule: the headline/support pair must explicitly name weight loss, obesity reduction, excess-weight reduction, appetite control for weight loss, or a 15-day weight outcome; if the standalone headline leads with tension/proof/system identity/deadline/feeling, the support line must anchor the weight-loss context clearly.
priority_block: Priority 1: lead with the main promise of fast visible weight loss and 3 to 5 kg in 15 days when the context allows it.
Priority 2: cravings down, hunger reduction, and easier caloric-deficit adherence are the main physical support benefits and should appear before routine mechanics.
Priority 3: natural safe-feeling, no-side-effects positioning, and all-natural Ayurvedic trust should be used when safety or harsh-method fear is relevant.
Priority 4: homemade-food compatibility and no-separate-meal-prep benefits should be used for time burden and family-food friction, but should not become the whole brand center.
Priority 5: structured system, low guesswork, and clear routine should be used when the pain is confusion, overwhelm, or repeated inconsistency.
Priority 6: guided support means tracker-led guidance, WhatsApp follow-up, and coach support, not vague emotional support copy.
Priority 7: experiential or digestive benefits can support the message, but should not overshadow the main promise.
Priority 8: AM routine and PM routine are mechanism lanes that should be used when the chosen angle genuinely needs morning or night protocol detail, not as the default lead every time.
Priority 9: headlines and subheadlines must stay feature-based and outcome-linked; do not convert them into generic transformation slogans.
Priority 10: if the detailed product doc defines a stronger feature hierarchy or interpretation rule, preserve that detail instead of compressing it.
Priority 11: headline style must not be forced into old extracted-xlsx patterns; the lane must come from product features first.
headline_strategy: Protected priority order for feature-led copy: fast visible results first; cravings down and appetite control for weight loss second; natural safe-feeling and no-side-effects third; homemade-food compatibility/no separate meal prep fourth; structured system/low guesswork fifth; guided support sixth; emotional-control/confidence-restoring can support but not replace main promise; digestive comfort/secondary internal benefits can support but should not lead by default; AM routine and PM routine only when angle genuinely needs morning/night protocol detail; proof/guarantee can support but should not flatten stronger benefit lanes above.

Headline and subheadline rules: headline oriented around concrete product features or product-mechanic outcomes; headline/support pair must explicitly name weight loss/obesity reduction/excess-weight reduction/appetite control for weight loss or a 15-day weight outcome; subheadline complements with a second feature lane (proof/routine/mechanism/event timing/ease/safety/guided support) and must not be a tiny rewording of the headline; hierarchy = headline (one sharp hook) + support line (reason to believe) + CTA (next step).

Avoid repetition: do not let all ads under the same format repeat the same headline skeleton/subheadline skeleton; rotate feature lanes across ads in the same format so the set does not collapse into one repetitive routine theme.

Concept variation framework: choose one audience stage + one lead angle + one message structure before writing; stage options = unaware/problem-aware/solution-aware/product-aware; lead angle options = pain point/desired outcome/social proof/authority/story/curiosity/comparison/offer; structure options = PAS/BAB/FAB/4U; use as direction only (not visible text); write fresh from product facts/persona pain/feature lane/proof points; do not copy prompt wording or previous ad wording into final headline.

Style guardrails: write human-edited lines not AI slogans; avoid unlock/transform your journey/revolutionary/holistic wellness/game-changing/effortlessly/tailored solution/vague transformation; prefer short spoken phrasing and natural line breaks; if it sounds like a spreadsheet label, rewrite as a real ad headline.

Format-level execution: HERO = one primary feature in headline + one secondary in support; UGC = creator-style practicality allowed but must keep a concrete feature lane; FEAT = each bullet is a different product feature; BA = left shows struggle/friction and right shows feature-led shift/progress state; TEST = review line references a specific feature/routine benefit/outcome driver (not vague praise).
trigger_rules: Do not default-lead with AM/PM routine; use AM routine and PM routine as mechanism lanes only when the chosen angle genuinely needs morning/night protocol detail.
Rotate feature lanes across ads in the same format to avoid collapsing into one repetitive routine theme.
Keep headlines/subheadlines feature-based and outcome-linked; avoid generic transformation slogans and old spreadsheet-like placeholder patterns.
Use guided support as tracker-led guidance + WhatsApp follow-up + coach support (not vague emotional support).
If safety/harsh-method fear is relevant, use natural safe-feeling/no-side-effects/all-natural Ayurvedic trust lane.
If time burden/family-food friction is relevant, use homemade-food compatibility/no separate meal prep lane but do not make it the brand center.
